dictionary_sections.definition: DNA/RNA Transition Lindblad Operators Duplicate is a source-backed registry entry whose production form is anchored to the reviewed equation \begin{aligned} L_{\mathrm{ribose}} &= \sqrt{\gamma_{r}}\, \lvert \mathrm{DNA} \rangle \langle \mathrm{RNA} \rvert \otimes \mathrm{Remove}(2^{\prime}\text{--OH}), \[4pt] L_{\mathrm{thymine}} &= \sqrt{\gamma_{t}}\, \lvert T \rangle \langle U \rvert \otimes \mathrm{Add}(\mathrm{CH}_{3}), \[4pt] L_{\mathrm{helix}} &= \sqrt{\gamma_{h}}\, \lvert \mathrm{B} \rangle \langle \mathrm{A} \rvert \otimes \mathrm{Twist}(36^{\circ} \to 34.3^{\circ}) . \end{aligned}. The entry records the mathematical object, condition, or operator named by the title and prepares it for the locked TZPID four-page layout.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: In context, DNA/RNA Transition Lindblad Operators Duplicate functions as a local mathematical checkpoint. The canonical equation gives the entry its printable formal spine, while the supporting equations show how the relation is used or constrained inside the surrounding registry arc.
